The witnesses agree the chapter opens by fixing the order of God's house: Aaron's line stands as the priests and Moses' among the common Levites, the greater of the two brothers in office being the elder; the genealogy is set down to establish who served at the altar and who about the tabernacle, the whole tribe ranked under God's appointment.

The witnesses agree the ground of the Levites' calling reaches back to the night of the Exodus: when God smote the firstborn of Egypt and spared Israel's, he claimed all Israel's firstborn as his own (Exod. 13:2); now he takes the tribe of Levi instead, the whole arrangement resting on redemption — a people delivered from death owing their firstborn, and a tribe given in their stead.
